items.title: World Gymnastics Opens Investigation Into Russian Athlete’s Conduct At World Cup
items.summary: The World Gymnastics federation will review the controversial incident involving Russian neutral athlete, Sofiia Ilteriakova, during an award ceremony at the 2026 Rhythmic Gymnastics World Cup in Sofia, Bulgaria.

items.title: Why The Home Spa Is The New Luxury Gift This Mother’s Day
items.summary: From LYMA Laser to HigherDOSE, Hello Klean, William Holland and Somavedic, Mother’s Day gifts are shifting toward home spa, recovery and wellbeing at home.

items.title: Pulisic’s Scoring Slump Is Concerning For Struggling U.S.
items.summary: Christian Pulisic hasn't scored for the USMNT in eight games, which is concerning because he is the team's best player prior to the World Cup.

items.title: If They Pay “Rewards” For Stablecoin Deposits, Then They’re Banks
items.summary: Imagine how competitive banks would and could be if not so hemmed in by rules that new entrants to the banking sector don’t have to abide.

items.title: How Bruce Campbell Almost Ended Up In Sam Raimi’s ‘Send Help’
items.summary: Bruce Campbell says that he and his "Evil Dead" franchise collaborator Sam Raimi discussed a cameo for Raimi’s new horror survival thriller "Send Help."

items.title: Iran’s President Says Country Has ‘No Enmity’ For Americans In Letter Posted Before Trump Speech
items.summary: The Iranian president called his country’s actions in the ongoing war a “measured response grounded in legitimate self-defense.”

items.title: The US Navy Needs More Aircraft Carriers – It’s All About The Base
items.summary: The U.S. Navy had been set to decommission the USS Nimitz next month. Instead, the oldest active nuclear-powered aircraft carrier will remain in service for another year

items.title: Why Buffalo Bills WR DJ Moore Is The Explosive Weapon Josh Allen Needs
items.summary: Buffalo Bills receiver DJ Moore has versatility, durability and explosiveness. Here's why Moore is the type of wideout that quarterback Josh Allen needs.

items.title: BTS Makes History Among Groups With A New No. 1 Hit In America
items.summary: BTS nearly ties both The Rolling Stones and the Bee Gees as "Swim" opens atop the Billboard Hot 100, earning the group a seventh career champion.

items.title: Ghislaine Maxwell Has ‘No Remorse’ For Epstein Crimes, ‘Housewives’ Star Jen Shah Claims
items.summary: Shah said she intentionally had only “limited interactions” with Maxwell when they were in the same Texas prison facility.

items.title: Where To Eat In London Right Now
items.summary: Five of London’s best under-the-radar restaurants right now. Thoughtful cooking, strong flavours and places foodies keep going back to.

items.title: Anthropic–Pentagon Dispute Brings A Turning Point For The AI Industry
items.summary: Anthropic and DoD are in a battle over the acceptable military use of AI models. The case highlights tensions between AI safety, ethics and national security.

items.title: Stage Is Set For ‘EPiC: Elvis Presley In Concert’ Streaming Release, Report Says
items.summary: "EPiC: Elvis Presley in Concert," director Baz Luhrmann’s acclaimed concert documentary, is reportedly coming soon to digital streaming.

items.title: Innocent Woman Arrested On Bogus AI Facial Recognition Match. The Failure Was Entirely Human
items.summary: Police jailed an innocent woman for five months on a bogus AI facial recognition match. Here's how the technology works and why the real failure is human, not machine.

items.title: Rideshare App Uber, Launches Uber Golf And Collaborates With Topgolf
items.summary: Rideshare app Uber launches Uber Golf, intended to help golf groups get to and from the course. Uber also announces a collaboration with Topgolf.
